competitor_patterns: Extreme word counts: top-ranking pages average 14,000–19,000 words (OutdoorGearLab: 16,573; BikeRumor: 19,302), far exceeding our 1,088–1,259 word posts — depth signals authority to Google
Structured comparison tables are universal among top 3 results (OutdoorGearLab, BikeRumor both use tables); our posts rarely include them, reducing scannability and featured-snippet eligibility

FAQ sections appear on every top-ranking competitor page, and our posts do include them — but competitor FAQs are longer (10+ questions) vs our implied shorter versions, likely capturing more PAA slots
Top competitors use 'tested & ranked' or 'we tested X models' framing in H1 and meta titles — signalling first-hand authority which Google heavily weights for YMYL/product categories in 2026

Competitor H2 structures segment by use-case (MTB, road, budget, performance) creating topical silos that capture multiple long-tail variants simultaneously; our H2s are argument-driven rather than query-driven
our_gaps: Word count deficit is critical: our posts average ~1,200 words vs competitor average of 14,000+ — we are unlikely to rank top 3 for competitive head terms without significantly deeper content (minimum 3,000–4,000 words for pillar posts)

No comparison tables in most posts: the 'Sigma vs Velluto' post has one table but other posts lack them entirely — tables directly increase featured snippet eligibility and dwell time, both ranking signals

Missing use-case segmentation: competitors rank for MTB, gravel, road, budget sub-queries by dedicating full H2 sections to each; our posts focus on a single use-case per article, missing topical breadth

No social proof or test methodology section: competitors explicitly state 'we tested X models over Y miles' — Velluto's 30-day trial is a unique advantage that could substitute for this but is not being leveraged as a credibility signal in content

Brand misspelling traffic ('veluto', 'vellutto') is not being captured with a dedicated landing page or redirect strategy — 299 impressions at 0.7% CTR for 'veluto' represents ~2 clicks vs potential 15+ with a corrected title tag
keyword_opportunities: best road bike glasses 2026 — competitor H2 'Best Road Bike Glasses' at OutdoorGearLab signals high-volume sub-query; no dedicated Velluto post exists

best value cycling sunglasses 2026 — OutdoorGearLab's H2 'Best Value Cycling Sunglasses' and 'Best Performance Sunglasses on a Tight Budget' target price-sensitive searchers where Velluto's pricing advantage is strongest
cycling sunglasses interchangeable lenses — a product-feature query where StradaPro's click-in lens system is a direct match; no competitor brand dominates this with a product-specific post
lightweight cycling glasses under 30g — ultra-specific query matching StradaPro's 25g frame exactly; likely low competition, high purchase intent
oakley sutro lite alternative 2026 — BikeRumor explicitly covers 'Oakley Sutro Lite' as a top pick, making 'Oakley Sutro Lite alternative' a high-intent comparison query Velluto is positioned to own
next_post_guidelines: Target minimum 3,000 words for any post competing on 'best cycling glasses 2026' or 'oakley alternatives' — match competitor depth by including Quick Picks summary at top, per-product deep-dive sections, and a buyer's guide conclusion

Structure H2s around search queries not arguments: use H2s like 'Best Cycling Glasses for Road Riding 2026', 'Best Lightweight Cycling Sunglasses', 'Best Oakley Alternative for Cyclists' to capture multiple PAA and long-tail variants in one post

Include a minimum 5-column comparison table for every product-comparison or roundup post: columns should be Weight, Lens System, Anti-Fog, Price, Trial Policy — this directly targets featured snippet formats Google shows for 'best X' queries

Add a 'How We Tested' section (even 150 words) referencing Velluto's 30-day trial rides across real conditions — this mirrors the credibility framing ('we tested X models') that differentiates top-ranking editorial content

Expand FAQ sections to 8–10 questions minimum, pulling directly from Google's People Also Ask for each target keyword before writing — prioritise questions like 'Are interchangeable lens cycling glasses worth it?' and 'What weight should cycling glasses be?'

For 'oakley alternatives' content (178 impressions, pos 7.0): lead with a direct feature-for-feature table vs Oakley Sutro Lite specifically, since BikeRumor ranks it as a top pick — position StradaPro's 25g weight and 30-day trial as the decisive differentiators

Use the current year '2026' in H1, meta title, and at least the first H2 for all roundup/comparison posts — all top-ranking competitors do this and it signals freshness to both Google and AI overview systems
seo_quick_wins: Add a sticky comparison table (Quick Picks box) within the first 300 words of every roundup post — OutdoorGearLab and BikeRumor both do this; it increases dwell time, reduces bounce, and is Google's preferred format for featured snippets on 'best X' queries

Implement FAQ schema markup (JSON-LD) on every post that already has an FAQ section — our posts already have FAQs but schema markup is likely missing, meaning we are not eligible for FAQ rich results that would double our SERP real estate

Add internal links from every new post back to the StradaPro product page using keyword-rich anchor text ('interchangeable lens cycling glasses', 'ultralight cycling sunglasses') — current posts appear editorially siloed from product pages, wasting link equity
meta_title_fixes: QUERY: 'veluto' (299 impressions, 0.7% CTR, pos 5.6) → New title: Velluto Cycling Glasses – Official Store | velluto-shop.com | Why: Users misspelling 'veluto' are looking for the brand; a clear brand-confirmation title with domain trust signal will lift CTR from 0.7% to estimated 4–6% by removing ambiguity about whether the result is the real brand

QUERY: 'best cycling sunglasses 2026' (192 impressions, 2.1% CTR, pos 8.9) → New title: Best Cycling Sunglasses 2026: Tested on Real Rides | Velluto | Why: Mirrors the 'tested' framing of OutdoorGearLab (#1 ranking) and adds the year upfront — both are proven CTR drivers for 'best X year' queries; keeps under 60 chars

QUERY: 'oakley alternatives' (178 impressions, 2.8% CTR, pos 7.0) → New title: Top Oakley Alternatives 2026 – Lighter & Cheaper | Velluto | Why: Leads with the user's pain point (find an alternative), adds '2026' for freshness, and surfaces Velluto's two strongest advantages (lighter, cheaper) as the click hook — targets higher purchase-intent clicks
content_quick_wins: QUERY: 'velluto' (pos 5.1, 1,118 impressions, 54 clicks) → Topic: Velluto StradaPro – Official Brand Hub Page | Add: Ensure the homepage or a dedicated brand landing page has StradaPro product name, key specs (25g, UV400, 30-day trial), and trust signals (Italian design, interchangeable lenses) in the first 100 words and title tag — brand queries at pos 5.1 should be ranking #1; likely a title tag or homepage authority issue

QUERY: 'best cycling glasses 2026' (pos 7.6, 139 impressions, 6 clicks) → Topic: Best Cycling Glasses 2026: Road, Gravel & MTB Tested | Add: Expand to 3,500+ words with use-case H2 sections (road, gravel, budget under €100, lightweight), a 5-column Quick Picks table, 10-question FAQ with schema markup, and explicit 'tested over 30 days' methodology — this mirrors the exact structure of pos 1–3 competitors

QUERY: 'oakley alternatives' (pos 7.0, 178 impressions, 5 clicks) → Topic: 5 Best Oakley Alternatives for Cyclists in 2026 (Lighter & Cheaper) | Add: Feature-for-feature table comparing StradaPro vs Oakley Sutro Lite vs Oakley Radar Eye on weight, lens swap ease, anti-fog, price, trial policy; add a 'Why we picked each alternative' section and a bottom CTA linking to 30-day trial — the comparison table alone can push this to top 3 for this query

QUERY: 'best cycling sunglasses 2026' (pos 8.9, 192 impressions, 4 clicks) → Topic: Best Cycling Sunglasses 2026: 12 Models Tested by Cyclists | Add: Increase depth to match BikeRumor's format — individual H2 for each shortlisted product, a Quick Picks summary box, purchase-intent CTAs after each product section, and a 'How to choose' buyer's guide at the bottom; also update meta title to include 'Tested' to match top-ranking titles

QUERY: 'oakley alternative' (pos 7.1, 52 impressions, 1 click) → Topic: Consolidate with 'oakley alternatives' post via canonical or redirect | Add: Target both singular and plural via one authoritative 3,000-word post; Google treats these as near-identical intent, splitting authority across two thin posts hurts both — combine and 301 redirect the weaker URL to the stronger one
geo_angles: Structure product and blog content around direct answer formats: lead every post with a 2–3 sentence summary box answering 'What are the best cycling glasses in 2026?' or 'Is Velluto a good Oakley alternative?' — AI Overviews and Perplexity preferentially cite pages that contain a clear, quotable answer within the first 150 words, matching the user's exact query phrasing

Create a dedicated 'Velluto StradaPro vs [Competitor]' comparison hub that AI tools can cite as a neutral-feeling source: structure it as 'StradaPro weighs 25g vs Oakley Sutro Lite at 26g', 'StradaPro includes a 30-day risk-free trial, Oakley does not' — specific, factual, comparative claims in table format are the content type most frequently extracted by ChatGPT and Perplexity for product queries

Publish a 'Velluto StradaPro: Full Specs & Review 2026' page using structured data (Product schema with weight, price, review rating, return policy) — Google AI Overviews increasingly pull from structured product data and pages with explicit spec callouts; including '25g', 'UV400', '30-day trial', 'interchangeable lenses' as labelled spec items (not just prose) increases inclusion probability in AI-generated answers
comparison_post_angles: COMPETITOR: OutdoorGearLab — angle: 'We Did the Testing So You Don't Have to: StradaPro vs the OutdoorGearLab Winners' — headline idea: 'Velluto StradaPro vs the Top-Rated Cycling Glasses of 2026: A Real-Rider Comparison' | key differentiator: OutdoorGearLab tests in lab conditions; Velluto offers a 30-day real-ride trial so the buyer becomes the tester | Velluto advantage to lead with: '30-day risk-free trial — test it on your actual route, not a test track'

COMPETITOR: BikeRumor — angle: 'BikeRumor Named the Oakley Sutro Lite a Top Pick — Here's Why StradaPro Is Lighter and Half the Price' — headline idea: 'Oakley Sutro Lite vs Velluto StradaPro 2026: Weight, Lenses, Value Compared' | key differentiator: BikeRumor's top pick (Oakley Sutro Lite) is heavier and lacks interchangeable lenses at the same price tier | Velluto advantage to lead with: '25g ultralight frame vs Sutro Lite's 26g, plus tool-free interchangeable lenses the Sutro Lite doesn't offer'

COMPETITOR: Cyclist.co.uk — angle: 'Cyclist.co.uk Recommends Oakley Sphaera — But Is Italian Design + Interchangeable Lenses a Better Deal?' — headline idea: 'Oakley Sphaera vs Velluto StradaPro: Which Road Cycling Glasses Win in 2026?' | key differentiator: Cyclist.co.uk audience is road-focused European cyclists — exactly Velluto's core customer; Sphaera has no lens swap system and no trial period | Velluto advantage to lead with: 'Click-in lens swap in seconds vs Sphaera's single-lens system — and Italian design at a fraction of Oakley's price'
